text.title: Change Control Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Change Control. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every change request MUST be logged in an immutable register with id, requester, timestamp, status, tier, and decision. Edits create new revisions; rows are never deleted.
rule.rationale: Without an immutable log there is no audit trail; scope-creep disputes become he-said-she-said.

rule.statement: Every CR MUST carry an impact analysis covering cost delta, schedule delta, scope delta, and risk delta BEFORE it reaches the approver tier.
rule.rationale: Approvers without impact data either approve blindly or stall indefinitely; both destroy project flow.

rule.statement: CRs MUST route to a tiered approver based on cost+schedule impact: tier-1 (PM, <2% baseline), tier-2 (sponsor, 2-10%), tier-3 (CCB, >10%).
rule.rationale: Single-approver designs either bottleneck on the sponsor or under-govern small changes; tiered design preserves both speed and rigour.

rule.statement: When a CR is approved, the project baseline MUST be re-versioned with the CR id recorded. The old baseline is retained read-only.
rule.rationale: Without versioned baselines, EVM calculations break and historical comparisons become impossible.

rule.statement: Rejected CRs MUST remain in the register with rejection rationale and rejecting approver. Silent deletion is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent rejection encourages re-submission with cosmetic changes; recorded rejection creates a teachable trail.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Change Control fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Change Control outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
